---
title: Installation
---

## Base install

Install from PyPI with `pip`:

```bash
pip install prisma-flow
```

or with `uv`:

```bash
uv add prisma-flow
```

The base install supports:

- JSON input and output
- SVG rendering
- standalone HTML rendering
- Mermaid text rendering
- validation reports
- the `prisma-flow` CLI

## Optional YAML support

```bash
pip install "prisma-flow[yaml]"
```

or:

```bash
uv add "prisma-flow[yaml]"
```

## Optional PNG support

PNG export is intentionally optional. In v0.1, `to_png()` and
`prisma-flow render --format png` raise a clear optional-dependency error rather
than adding a required rendering stack.

```bash
uv add "prisma-flow[png]"
```

## Development install

```bash
conda env create -f conda/dev.yaml
conda activate prismaflow
poetry config virtualenvs.create false
poetry install --extras "dev yaml"
```

Run quality checks through Makim:

```bash
makim tests.linter
makim tests.unit
makim package.build
makim docs.build
```

## Documentation tooling

The documentation site is built with [Quarto](https://quarto.org/). If you use
Conda, install the development environment:

```bash
conda env create -f conda/dev.yaml
conda activate prismaflow
```

Build the docs:

```bash
quarto render docs
```
